data.title: FSB Chair Warns of Growing Systemic Risks in Crypto-Finance Integration
data.description:
According to PANews, Klaas Knot, the Chair of the Financial Stability Board (FSB), highlighted the increasing systemic risks posed by the deepening integration of cryptocurrencies with the traditional financial system during a speech at the Bank of Spain conference. Knot noted that the introduction of cryptocurrency ETFs and the substantial holdings of U.S. Treasury bonds by stablecoin issuers have significantly lowered the barriers for retail investors, accelerating the accumulation of systemic risks.

He cautioned that the rapid innovation in the cross-border payments sector, coupled with inadequate regulation, could amplify risk transmission. Knot stressed the importance of countries expediting the implementation of FSB regulatory recommendations to establish a coordinated regulatory framework, given the cross-border nature of cryptocurrencies.

This speech marked Knot's final major address as FSB Chair, where he also addressed the risks of leverage and liquidity mismatches in the non-bank financial intermediation sector.

data.title: Ethereum Breakout Imminent? Broadening Wedge Hints At $4,200 Surge
data.description:
According to recent technical analysis, Ethereum (ETH) may be gearing up for a major breakout that could propel the cryptocurrency close to the $4,200 mark. Meanwhile, ETH continues to attract growing institutional interest, with Ethereum exchange-traded funds (ETFs) outperforming their Bitcoin (BTC) counterparts. Ethereum Headed For A Breakout? In a recent X post, noted crypto analyst Titan of Crypto highlighted that ETH is climbing within a massive weekly broadening wedge structure. The analyst shared the following chart and suggested that ETH could be targeting the $4,200 level – marking the top of the wedge
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/ethereum-news/ethereum-breakout-imminent-broadening-wedge-hints-at-4200-surge/

data.title: Market Sentiment Remains Bearish as Funding Rates Indicate Downtrend
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, data from Coinglass reveals that the current funding rates on major centralized and decentralized exchanges indicate a bearish market sentiment. The funding rates for key cryptocurrencies are shown in the accompanying data.

Funding rates are fees set by cryptocurrency trading platforms to maintain a balance between contract prices and the prices of the underlying assets, typically applied to perpetual contracts. This mechanism facilitates the exchange of funds between long and short traders, without the platform charging this fee, to adjust the cost or profit of holding contracts, ensuring that contract prices remain close to the underlying asset prices.

A funding rate of 0.01% represents the benchmark rate. When the funding rate exceeds 0.01%, it indicates a generally bullish market sentiment. Conversely, when the funding rate falls below 0.005%, it suggests a predominantly bearish market outlook.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETF had a total net inflow of $86.3114 million yesterday, continuing its net inflow for 4 consecutive days
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to SoSoValue data, yesterday (June 12, Eastern Time), the total net inflow of Bitcoin spot ETFs was 86.3114 million US dollars.
The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest net inflow in a single day yesterday was Blackrock ETF IBIT, with a net inflow of 288 million US dollars in a single day. Currently, IBIT's total net inflow in history has reached 49.527 billion US dollars.
The second is Grayscale ETF GBTC, with a net inflow of 5.8919 million US dollars in a single day, and the total net outflow of GBTC in history has reached 23.248 billion US dollars.
The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest net outflow in a single day yesterday was Fidelity ETF FBTC, with a net outflow of 197 million US dollars in a single day, and the total net inflow of FBTC in history has reached 11.493 billion US dollars.
As of press time, the total net asset value of the Bitcoin spot ETF is US$130.263 billion, the ETF net asset ratio (market value to the total market value of Bitcoin) is 6.18%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached US$45.309 billion.

data.title: A whale once again transferred $6.55 million worth of PEPE to Binance, with a total floating loss of $3.26 million
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale further transferred $6.55 million worth of PEPE to Binance. Overall, the whale deposited 1.6T PEPE worth $18.1 million to Binance and faced a loss of $3.26 million. The whale still has 600 billion PEPE in its wallet, worth $6.39 million.

data.title: SmartMall released a new series of NFTs today and simultaneously launched the "Group Buying" model
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to official reports, SmartMall, a Web3 smart application aggregation traffic platform, released a new series of NFTs today and simultaneously launched a "group purchase" model. Users can form teams to purchase collectively at a lower price. After the group purchase is successful, team members can also unlock additional rewards.
